PrusaSlicer – the 3D-printing slicer, in your browser, for Unraid

Have your model and slice it too — no VNC client, no local install.

   

What is this?

The full PrusaSlicer desktop app — the FDM/SLA slicer from Prusa Research — running in one container and streamed to your browser over Selkies (WebRTC). Open the WebUI and slice: no X client, no VNC viewer, nothing to install on your workstation. The WebRTC stream keeps the 3D plate smooth to rotate, zoom and drag — the part of slicing where the older noVNC containers feel laggy.

LinuxServer ship OrcaSlicer and Cura on Selkies but no PrusaSlicer, and the only other browser-based PrusaSlicer images are years-old, abandoned noVNC builds. This is a maintained, modern Selkies build for amd64 and arm64.

Highlights

  • The real PrusaSlicer desktop, streamed over WebRTC (Selkies) — smooth 3D plate, native Prusa printer/filament profiles
  • Installed from Debian trixie's prusa-slicer package (PrusaSlicer no longer ships a Linux AppImage), so it tracks Debian security updates
  • No login by default for trusted-LAN use — set a WebUI username + password to enable HTTP basic auth
  • Dark by default — dark GTK chrome; PrusaSlicer's own Dark mode is under Preferences → General
  • Profiles & configuration persist under /config; your models and G-code live under /storage
  • GPU-accelerated when a GPU is passed through, software rendering otherwise (works either way)
  • Multi-arch — amd64 + arm64
  • Image: junkerderprovinz/prusaslicer:latest (Docker Hub / GHCR)

Requirements

  • Unraid 6.12+
  • Open the WebUI over HTTPS on port 3001 (self-signed cert — accept it once). Port 3000 (HTTP) is for a reverse proxy that terminates TLS, not direct browser access
  • Mount your models / G-code folder to /storage (defaults to /mnt/user) so imports and slices land on your array
  • For the smoothest 3D viewport, pass through a GPU; without one it falls back to software rendering, which still works

Posting a bug report

Please post:

  • Unraid version (Settings → System Information)
  • Image tag (latest or a pinned vX.Y.Z)
  • Output of docker logs --tail 200 PrusaSlicer
  • Browser + OS you're using
  • Whether you changed the ports, the /config / /storage mounts, or set a WebUI username/password

GitHub issues with the same info are also welcome: github.com/junkerderprovinz/prusaslicer/issues

Credits

Packages PrusaSlicer by Prusa Research (AGPL-3.0) on LinuxServer.io's baseimage-selkies, streamed with Selkies. Not affiliated with or endorsed by Prusa Research. If this saves you a slicer setup, you can buy me a coffee.

Self-hosted, MIT-licensed wrapper — you run it, you own your data and the responsibility.
